The correct choice is **D** because it provides the most balanced dimensions to safely handle the torque while respecting the given shear and bearing stress limits. The wider key (3/4 in.) increases bearing area, while the thicker section (1/2 in.) enhances shear resistance, ensuring the design meets the 30,000 lbs-in. torque requirement. The length (2.5 in.) also optimizes engagement with the shaft.
